---
title: "Paraquat Sensitivity Test"
type: "protocol"
---
# Paraquat Sensitivity Test

**Resources:** [[paraquat]], 

**People:** [[grey-monroe]], [[satoyo-oya]], [[jiani-li]]

**SOP:** [[hazardous-drug-sop]]

### Objective

Test the sensitivity of msh6, modified MSH6s and atx1/2/r7 mutants to the putative oxidizing mutagen paraquat.

### Note on Paraquat

Synonym: Methyl viologen dichloride hydrate  257.16 g/mol 
Product: Sigma 856177-1G ([Safety Data Sheet](https://www.sigmaaldrich.com/US/en/sds/ALDRICH/856177?srsltid=AfmBOorJwo_PvCCQ0RnGouRQ9JBRcGAlXLUl23QXr5-HiPbv784yefgE))
Toxic! Work under the Chemical hood, wear PPE (double layers of nitrile gloves, lab coats, face shield). Dispose gloves and wash your hands well after handling. Never drain to the sink. Contaminated media/plasticware should be disposed of as hazardous waste ( in the large plastic bag in the right back of the chemical hood. 

Prosperity
Very soluble in water. Stable in acid or neutral solutions but decomposes in the presence of UV light, unstable in alkaline solutions, inactivated by inert clays and ionic surfactants. So in cases of spill, some alkaline solutions or SDS (the detergent you are familiar with) may be used to decontaminate the surface. In case of spills 
When is it safe to touch?
![](data:image/png;base64,)
PEL = Permissible Exposure Limit (PEL), are the concentration of a hazardous substance in the air averaged over an 8-hour workday and a 40-hour workweek to which it is believed that workers may be repeatedly exposed, day after day, for a working lifetime without adverse effects. 
Our working solution ; 750 µM solution = 0.193 mg/ml.  2 µM media= 514.32 mg/m^3.  Therefore these are well above PEL. Never touch directly. When 2 µM is diluted to 1/1000, okay to touch.

1.5 M stock solution can be made by adding 2.6 ml of UPW to 1G of Paraquat, and it should be stored in the mutagen container in the chemical hood. The stock solution is stable at room temperature.

Before starting the project

* Make Waste tag/bottle
* Make the 1.5 M stock solution
* Go through what kill curve looks like
* Locations of the seeds (I added the seeds in the seed box on your bench)
* Add paraquat on SOP (Asking Kehan)

### Overview of the procedure

Firstly, do an experiment with only WT to get a “kill curve”. This is a pilot experiment to determine the right dose of paraquat and right timing to score the phenotype. Apply various doses of paraquat to WT. If the dose is too high, the plants will die. If the dose is too small, there will be no detectable phenotype. If you score The objective is to identify a dose that nearly halves the phenotype score;
Once you know the right dose from the kill curve experiment, apply that dose to WT and mutants, and score the phenotype. Replicate this twice.

### Detailed Procedure for True Leaf Extension Assay

#### Kill curve

Material

* WT (23/198-2)
* Normal MS plates
* Normal MS media
* Paraquat (1.5 M stock)
* Forceps
* A 2 ml tube (to make 750 µM working solution)
* A bottle of 0.1 % SDS solution (for decontamination)
* Sterilized 6 well plates (@ the shelf behind the chemical hood)
* Disposable pipet (@ plasticwares shelf)

Prepare plants

* Plate WT seeds (about 15 seeds par genotype/condition- so in this case 75 seeds or more) on normal MS media and stratify for 2 days in the fridge.
* Move the plants to the incubator and let them grow, until the tiny cotyledons are just appearing and not fully turning green. This is usually achievable by 2 days of incubation for WT, perhaps 3 days for other mutants.

Prepare media

* Make liquid MS media, autoclave and let it come to RT. 6 ml x number of samples is sufficient. So in this case 30 ml is sufficient.

Mutagenize/growth

* In a clean bench, aliquot 5 ml of MS media to each well of sterilized six-well culture plate. Transfer the plants (15 plants/well) from the plates to well. Avoid too fast or too slowly growing plant individuals.
* Move to chemical hood.  Dilute the 1.5 M paraquat solution (1 µl paraquat in 2 ml water) to make a 750 µM working solution.
* Add the diluted 7.5 mM paraquat to the wells.

| dose (µM) | Add this amount to 5 ml media (µl) |
| --------- | ---------------------------------- |
| 0 | 0 |
| 0.25 | 1.67 |
| 0.5 | 3.33 |
| 1 | 6.67 |
| 2 | 13.3 |

* Seal the well plates with surgical tape.
* Place the well plates in the chemical hood with a light on.
* Check on the plates regularly (every day or every two days) for the appearance of true leaves. When the dosage response is clear (= 100% of plants having true leaves in low to zero dose, and high dose not or rarely have true leaves, capture the image. Record how many days have passed since plants were placed in the liquid media.
* [Capturing image] Open the well plate, and remove the media from wells using disposable pipette. Discard the liquid media to the liquid mutagen waste bottle.
* Using forceps within the chemical hood, move and arrange the plants so that they are not overlapping for the image capture
* Bring the plate out of the chemical hood. Scan the plates from the bottom as .jpeg image with 900 dpi, white background. In case of a spill, absorb the spill with paper towels, wipe once with SDS 0.1 % solution, then wipe twice again with water. Dispose the paper towels in the hazardous waste.
* After the image capture, dispose of the plates in the hazardous waste trash bag in the chemical hood.
* Fill in the following table from the image.

| Dose (µM) | Number of plants with true leaves | Total number of plants |
| --------- | --------------------------------- | ---------------------- |
| 0 |  |  |
| 0.25 |  |  |
| 0.5 |  |  |
| 1 |  |  |
| 2 |  |  |

#### Test on mutants

Plant Materials

| genotype | Name on the seed envelope |
| -------- | ------------------------- |
| WT | 23/198-2 |
| atx12r7 inaline | 24/87-0 Gy, "25-55" |
| atx12r7 newline | 24/99-1 |
| msh6 | 23/103-3\~5 mix |
| 3x FLAG MSH6 |  |
| 3x FLAG MSH6\_delTUDOR |  |
| 3x FLAG MSH6\_mutTUDOR |  |
| 3x FLAG MSH6\_AGD5/6 |  |

Prepare plants

* Plate 30 seeds per line on normal MS media and stratify for 2 days in the fridge.
* Move the plants to the incubator and let them grow, first plates until the tiny cotyledons are just appearing and not fully turning green. This is usually achievable by 2 days of incubation for WT.

Mutagenize/growth

* Proceed as before.
